video:video.video:title: Philippine motorists queue outside petrol stations in Manila amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: Oil companies in the Philippines announced a large pump price hike starting Tuesday. Prices are expected to rise by 17 to 24 pesos ($0.30 to $0.40) per litre.

video:video.video:title: How Pfeiffer and Russell worked on 'The Madison' through scheduling challenges
video:video.video:description: Stars Michelle Pfeiffer and Kurt Russell discuss making Taylor Sheridan's latest show "The Madison," at its premiere in New York. (March 10)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 10
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, March 10th: Trump says Iran war could be over quickly; More attacks launched at Persian Gulf region, Israel; Remains of U.S. soldier returned to U.S.; Long lines at some U.S. airports.

video:video.video:title: One killed and eight wounded in attack by Iran on Bahrain's capital
video:video.video:description: The attack comes a day after a drone attack injured 32 people, including several children, early Monday in the Sitra area near Manama, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth warns Tuesday will be the 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran'
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warns that Tuesday “will be yet again our most intense day of strikes inside Iran: The most fighters, the most bombers, the most strikes, intelligence more refined and better than ever.”

video:video.video:title: Large explosion hits Lebanese capital Beirut
video:video.video:description: A large explosion hit the Lebanese capital, Beirut, on Tuesday as Israel’s air force struck a southern suburb.

video:video.video:title: Mechanical excavator clears rubble after Israeli strikes in Beirut's southern suburbs
video:video.video:description: Excavators cleared debris from a building belonging to Hezbollah’s financial arm, the al-Qard Al-Hasan Association, in Beirut’s southern suburbs on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Crews battle fire aboard scrap metal barge
video:video.video:description: The barge is burning in the Delaware River near New Castle, Delaware

video:video.video:title: Stanford’s Ebuka Okorie on hard work paying off
video:video.video:description: Stanford’s Ebuka Okorie on hard work paying off

video:video.video:title: G7 nations consider tapping strategic oil reserves amid Iran war tensions
video:video.video:description: The Group of Seven (G7) are teeing up for potential use of their strategic oil reserves after their ministers met twice in two days to prepare such a move to bring down prices inflamed by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Stanford’s Ebuka Okorie on recruiting process
video:video.video:description: Stanford’s Ebuka Okorie on recruiting process

video:video.video:title: Top-secret Cold War nuclear bunker becomes a popular tourist attraction in Albania
video:video.video:description: A spartan nuclear bunker built during the Cold War rule of the late communist dictator Enver Hoxha could have become a forgotten relic of his communist regime, instead it's become a tourist hotspot.

video:video.video:title: Canadian police investigate gunfire at United States consulate in Toronto
video:video.video:description: Royal Canadian Mounted Police Chief Superintendent Chris Leather said the American and Israeli consulates, as well as embassies in Ottawa, will see an increase in security.

video:video.video:title: On heels of attempted attack, Mamdani says 'extremism of any kind will not be tolerated' in NYC
video:video.video:description: Authorities said counterprotesters allegedly attempted to detonate two improvised explosive devices during a protest near the mayor's home over the weekend.

video:video.video:title: At least 6 killed, 37 injured in Ukrainian attack on Russia’s Bryansk
video:video.video:description: At least six people were killed and 37 were injured in a Ukrainian missile attack on Russia’s Bryansk region, local governor Alexander Bogomaz said in a post on social media.

video:video.video:title: White House says US Navy hasn't escorted any tankers through the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt says a social media post by Energy Secretary Chris Wright - which was quickly taken down - claiming the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Judge to decide if Penn must produce records in probe of antisemitism at Ivy League school
video:video.video:description: Penn objects to federal judge demands for lists revealing workers’ Jewish faith, ancestry or ties to Jewish organizations. (AP Video: Tassanee Vejpongsa)

video:video.video:title: Video released by the Ukraine military said to show strike on plant in Russia's Bryansk region
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian missiles struck the city of Bryansk in western Russia on Tuesday, killing at least six civilians and injuring 37, regional Gov. Alexander Bogomaz said.

video:video.video:title: Greenpeace stages "No to war" protest in Madrid
video:video.video:description: Protesters opposed to the Iran war climbed onto one of the buildings in the famous Puerta del Sol square in the Spanish capital on Tuesday morning to display a large banner reading “No to war” as commuters were passing by on their way to work.

video:video.video:title: Large explosion hits Beirut
video:video.video:description: A large explosion hit the Lebanese capital, Beirut, on Tuesday as Israel’s air force struck a southern suburb.

video:video.video:title: UK warship departs for Cyprus amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: The U.K. warship HMS Dragon set sail from Portsmouth on Tuesday and is on its way to Cyprus. The 200-strong crew could be seen standing onboard the Royal Navy destroyer as the ship left the dock.

video:video.video:title: Zohran Mamdani condemns extremism after explosive devices found near the Mayor's home
video:video.video:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani says ‘extremism of any kind will not be tolerated’ as officials investigate what they call ISIS-inspired terrorism after two unexploded devices were found near his residence during a protest.

video:video.video:title: Wildflowers are blooming in the driest place in North America — but not for long
video:video.video:description: Video Journalist Ty ONeil goes to Death Valley National Park in California to take in a rare phenomenon known as a superbloom. (Video by: Ty ONeil)

video:video.video:title: Senators weigh U.S. role in Iran war as conflict enters second week
video:video.video:description: The Iran war entered its 11th day with no end in sight, as its effects were felt across the region and beyond.

video:video.video:title: Two high profile real estate brokers and their brother convicted drug, rape charges, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Oren, Alon and Tal Alexander have been convicted of charges accusing them of conspiring to drug and rape multiple women.

video:video.video:title: A small memorial is set up in Tel Aviv for six U.S. soldiers killed in a drone strike in Kuwait
video:video.video:description: The six members of the Army Reserve, who worked in logistics and kept troops supplied with food and equipment, died when a drone hit a command center in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, one day after the U.S. and Israel launched their military campaign against Iran.

video:video.video:title: As heat rises, so do complaints about stuffy subway rides
video:video.video:description: As temperatures rise above ground, the number of subway riders reporting uncomfortable heat belowground increases, according to a new study in the journal Nature Cities on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Baltimore police officer wounded, gunman killed during burglary call
video:video.video:description: A police officer responding to a burglary call in Baltimore was shot in the leg Tuesday by a gunman who was killed by another officer, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Why Alysa Liu and Eileen Gu’s Olympic choices are sparking debate
video:video.video:description: Alysa Liu and Eileen Gu have similar backgrounds but compete for different countries — the U.S. and China — a choice that has sparked debate online.

video:video.video:title: Jamie Lee Curtis, Nicole Kidman's friendship brought 'Scarpetta' to life
video:video.video:description: Jamie Lee Curtis and Nicole Kidman have brought "Scarpetta," the popular book series from Patricia Cornwell, to the screen after decades of attempts from Hollywood.

video:video.video:title: Mississippi voters discuss the issues behind their primary choices
video:video.video:description: Mississippi voters will choose party nominees for Congress in a state primary Tuesday. Voters in Jackson talked about the issues they care about most as they arrived at the polls.

video:video.video:title: Pope accepts resignation of US bishop who was arrested for alleged financial crimes
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has accepted the resignation of the Chaldean Catholic bishop of San Diego, California, a decision announced Tuesday by the Vatican after the bishop was arrested on embezzlement and money laundering charges.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, March 10th: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday would be the most intense day of strikes in Iran yet; explosive materials found in a Pennsylvania storage unit after homemade bombs thrown in New York; Mississippi Democrats to decide between U.S.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano erupts, shooting towering lava fountains
video:video.video:description: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano is shooting lava fountains up to 1,000 feet, and falling ash and volcanic fragments are forcing temporary closures on the Big Island.

video:video.video:title: Israel vows to dismantle Hezbollah completely, says President Herzog
video:video.video:description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog said on Tuesday that "Israel is determined to dismantle" the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah "completely."

video:video.video:title: Sleepy baby hippo yawns
video:video.video:description: Footage from John Ball Zoo in Grand Rapids, Michigan, shows Hugo, a baby pygmy hippo born Sept. 3, 2025, sleepily yawning.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Iran war causes growing pressure on oil production in region
video:video.video:description: Iran has effectively stopped tankers from using the Strait of Hormuz, which connects the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman — the gateway to the Indian Ocean — through which 20% of the world's oil is carried.

video:video.video:title: Stranded traveler in Doha abandons airline wait and drives to Saudi Arabia to reach home
video:video.video:description: An Indian technology entrepreneur stranded in Qatar during the recent Middle East airspace closures has finally made it home after an improvised journey by road and air that took more than two days.

video:video.video:title: And the Oscars afterparty goes to… Wolfgang Puck
video:video.video:description: The first stop for Oscar winners and invited guests after the show ends is the Governors Ball. Celebrity chef Wolfgang Puck is in his 32nd year catering the party, supported by 75 savory chefs and 45 pastry chefs.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's fragrant flower lei face competition from cheaper imports
video:video.video:description: Some Hawaii lawmakers think the state should do more to help producers of lei made with locally grown, fragrant flowers, rather than imported purple orchids, but some lei sellers worry that proposed rules would make the garlands too expensive.

video:video.video:title: Paraguay lawmakers approve a defense agreement that allows an increased US military presence
video:video.video:description: Paraguay’s Chamber of Deputies on Tuesday approved a defense agreement allowing the temporary presence of U.S. military and civilian personnel inside its borders, widely seen as a victory for the Trump administration, which has sought to strengthen its presence in Latin America.

video:video.video:title: Uncleared piles of trash bring health risks to Palestinians in the Gaza Strip
video:video.video:description: More than two million people in the Gaza Strip are struggling to protect their health as they live near piles of trash mixed with dirt and debris across the enclave, with limited resources to clear what were once vibrant, densely populated areas filled with bustling markets.

video:video.video:title: Funding lapse puts airport security, and privatization debate, into the spotlight
video:video.video:description: Aviation experts point to San Francisco International Airport as a model for insulating screening from funding gaps through private contractors, while critics warn privatization could put profits ahead of passenger safety.

video:video.video:title: A bus fire that killed 6 people could have been set deliberately, Swiss police say
video:video.video:description: Police are investigating whether a bus fire in a town west of the Swiss capital that killed at least six people was started deliberately.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutor says Rihanna, A$AP Rocky, 3 kids were home when woman charged with attempted murder fired
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a Florida woman has been charged with attempted murder and other felonies over gunshots fired at Rihanna's Los Angeles home last weekend.

video:video.video:title: At Palace event, Queen Camilla calls for support of abuse survivors
video:video.video:description: Queen Camilla reiterated her support for survivors of physical and sexual abuse at an event hosted at St James's Palace on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Soldiers train to belong to the Mexican army's special forces to combat criminal organization
video:video.video:description: Mexican soldiers training to be a part of the Special Forces Corps hiked up the Iztaccíhuatl volcano surrounding Mexico City on Tuesday as part of tests to become part of the army's toughest unit.

video:video.video:title: North Korea stages second strategic cruise missile test in a week, with Kim and daughter watching
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and his teenage daughter observed tests of strategic cruise missiles fired from a warship, state media reported Wednesday, as North Korea threatened responses to U.S.

video:video.video:title: More Israeli-US strikes rock the Iranian capital
video:video.video:description: Damage and debris were scattered near Tehran's Resalat Square following more strikes on the Iranian capital on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Air raid siren sound in Tel Aviv as Iranian missiles launch towards Israel
video:video.video:description: Air raid sirens sounded and interceptions were seen across Tel Aviv on Tuesday as Iranian missiles were launched towards Israel.

video:video.video:title: Do you really need a water filter?
video:video.video:description: Experts say most U.S. tap water is fine without a filter. But if you still want one for reassurance, here are some tips.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 11
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, March 11th: U.S. says it destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels; Pentagon says 140 U.S. military personnel wounded in war in Iran; Missile interceptions seen over Tel Aviv; Georgia special election for U.S.

video:video.video:title: Shipping vessels line up as Iran disrupts traffic in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Ships were seen lining up in front of Ras Al-Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday as Iran disrupted traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: US military says it destroyed 16 Iranian minelayers near the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The U.S. said it took out more than a dozen mine-laying Iranian vessels Tuesday, and the Islamic Republic vowed to block the region's oil exports, saying it would not allow “even a single liter” to be shipped to its enemies.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut sending clouds of smoke into the sky
video:video.video:description: Airstrikes could be seen on Beirut's skyline on Tuesday night and Wednesday morning as Israel stepped up its attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Beyond the Potomac River, sewage spills threaten cities with old infrastructure and little funds
video:video.video:description: A major sewer pipe collapse has dumped hundreds of millions of gallons of sewage into the Potomac River, highlighting a nationwide problem involving failing infrastructure.

video:video.video:title: Tornadoes cause significant damage in parts of Illinois and Indiana
video:video.video:description: Major storms that whipped up tornadoes in parts of Illinois and Indiana on Tuesday leveled homes, downed trees and power lines, and overwhelmed a 911 center south of Chicago with emergency calls, according to officials.

video:video.video:title: Italian government pays 30 million euros for rare Caravaggio portrait
video:video.video:description: Italy has bought a rare portrait by baroque painter Caravaggio for 30 million euros (about $35 million), the country’s Culture Ministry said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Progressive states revive push to tax the rich as wealth disparity deepens across the US
video:video.video:description: Advocates hope a growing unhappiness over wage and wealth inequalities that have increased since the COVID-19 pandemic will lead more U.S. states to adopt policies raising taxes on the wealthy.

video:video.video:title: War on Iran boils down to a single question: Who can take the pain the longest? AP explains
video:video.video:description: The ongoing Israeli-American war on Iran, for all its complexity and global effects, boils down to a single question: Who can take the pain the longest?

video:video.video:title: Human waste builds up in basements as old US infrastructure struggles to cope
video:video.video:description: The January collapse of a pipe dumped huge amounts of sewage into the Potomac River. But smaller sewer overflows that draw far less notice are common and sometimes cause backups into homes that threaten human health

video:video.video:title: Dozens of Christian Lebanese evacuated, gather at church for funeral of man killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: The United Nations peacekeeping mission in southern Lebanon, known as UNIFIL, helped almost 100 residents leave Alma al-Shaab early Tuesday, the last group of residents who for days tried to stand their ground amid the ongoing bombardments between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group.

video:video.video:title: Protesters block bus believed to be carrying Iran women's soccer team in Australia
video:video.video:description: Australia granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women’s soccer team who were visiting the country for a tournament when the Iran war began, a government minister said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Man taken into custody after driving into security gate outside White House, authorities say
video:video.video:description: A man was taken into custody on Wednesday after driving into a security barrier at the White House, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Vehicle crashes through White House barrier, investigation underway
video:video.video:description: Officials say a van drove through a barricade near the White House early Wednesday morning. Local media reports Metropolitan police and Secret Service responded to the vicinity of the White House around 6:30 a.m. after a van drove through a barricade at Connecticut Avenue and H Street.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan struggles to mitigate the fuel crisis in wake of Iran-US and Israel conflict
video:video.video:description: The war with Iran is doing collateral damage to the world economy. Pakistan finds itself in an especially bleak position.

video:video.video:title: Fuel shortages in India and Bangladesh impacting workers amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: India has invoked emergency powers requiring refiners to divert more fuel for domestic use, a move hospitality sector groups say has tightened supplies for restaurants amid the ongoing U.S.

video:video.video:title: Kankakee mayor discusses tornado that tore through town
video:video.video:description: Mayor of Illinois city discusses tornado damage

video:video.video:title: Savy King is 'grateful' to be able to play again
video:video.video:description: That Savy King not only survived a life-threatening on-field cardiac event but is now poised to return this season for Angel City is nothing short of miraculous, and she knows it.

video:video.video:title: Savy King recalls life-threatening cardiac event
video:video.video:description: During the second half of Angel City’s home match against the Utah Royals last May 9, King sat on the field in the 74th minute, appearing dazed.

video:video.video:title: Savy King doesn't want her cardiac event to define her
video:video.video:description: King said there are no restrictions on her and she does not need special monitoring or tests. She has no fear about continuing her playing career.

video:video.video:title: A drone looking for a missing man in Estonia finds a bear awake after hibernation
video:video.video:description: Estonian non-for-profit group MTÜ L.E.I.A was looking for a man reported missing near Tartu, when they spotted a bear playing in the snow.

video:video.video:title: Files show British prime minister was warned of ‘reputational risk’ in appointing Mandelson
video:video.video:description: Documents released by the British government show officials believed there was a “reputational risk” to appointing Peter Mandelson as the U.S. ambassador because of his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

video:video.video:title: Man in custody after driving vehicle into White House security gate
video:video.video:description: A man was taken into custody on Wednesday after driving into a security barrier at the White House, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Northwest Indiana resident hid in bathtub as deadly tornado hit neighborhood
video:video.video:description: Major storms whipped up tornadoes that killed at least two people and damaged homes in northwest Indiana.

video:video.video:title: Georgia voters choosing a successor to Marjorie Taylor Greene in US House
video:video.video:description: Months after Marjorie Taylor Greene resigned from Congress following a falling-out with President Donald Trump, voters in her Georgia district headed to the polls Tuesday to choose her successor in a special election.

video:video.video:title: Members of the UN peacekeeping force in Lebanon call for cessation of hostilities
video:video.video:description: Contributing countries to the UN peacekeeping force in Lebanon, were joined by other member states on Wednesday, to express deep alarm at the escalation of hostilities in Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Drone strike kills UN aid worker in the eastern Congo city of Goma
video:video.video:description: A series of explosions attributed to drones killed at least one person, a U.N. aid worker, as the attack on the Wednesday rocked downtown Goma in eastern Congo, according to the M23 rebel group and local residents.

video:video.video:title: Iran war has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil chokepoint. Reopening it is a big challenge
video:video.video:description: Gasoline prices are rising largely because of the Iran war's impact on the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial passageway for oil and gas from the Persian Gulf.

video:video.video:title: US Sen. Jon Husted testifies remotely in ex-FirstEnergy executives' corruption trial
video:video.video:description: Republican U.S. Sen. Jon Husted testified Wednesday that he was present at a 2018 dinner with Ohio’s then-Gov.

video:video.video:title: Oscars host Conan O'Brien helps roll out the red carpet
video:video.video:description: Academy Awards host Conan O'Brien joined crew members to roll out the red carpet for Sunday's Oscars at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: Iran war likely to cause Inflation to spike, consumer costs to soar in coming months, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Inflation stayed stubbornly elevated last month as gas prices rose in a snapshot of what consumer prices looked like before the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Video appears to show New York City bomb suspect buying fuse at a fireworks store
video:video.video:description: Newly released surveillance video appears to show a man buying a fuse at a fireworks store days before authorities say he and another man brought homemade bombs to a protest outside the New York City mayor’s residence.

video:video.video:title: Starmer warned of ‘reputational risk’ in appointing Mandelson but ignored red flags, files show
video:video.video:description: Starmer fired Mandelson after nine months in the job when new details of the relationship with Epstein emerged, and now faces a political storm over the appointment.

video:video.video:title: Casino donates food to unpaid TSA workers in Las Vegas as shutdown continues
video:video.video:description: MGM Resorts brought lunch for TSA workers in Las Vegas as the partial government shutdown continues. (AP Video by Ty ONeil)

video:video.video:title: Epstein's accountant Richard Kahn on Capitol Hill answering questions in closed door deposition
video:video.video:description: Richard Kahn, who worked closely with Jeffrey Epstein for years and now serves as an executor of his estate, appeared for the closed-door deposition on Capitol Hill before the House Oversight Committee.

video:video.video:title: Miami Heat's Adebayo scores 83 points, surpassing Kobe Bryant's record
video:video.video:description: Bam Adebayo had a night for all time on Tuesday, with a point total second to only Wilt Chamberlain in the NBA record books.

video:video.video:title: Statues of Founding Fathers added to Trump's revamped Rose Garden
video:video.video:description: Life-size figures of two U.S. Founding Fathers have been added to President Donald Trump's revamped Rose Garden at the White House as the country celebrates its 250th anniversary this year.

video:video.video:title: Why some airports might replace TSA agents with private contractors
video:video.video:description: Some U.S. airports use private contractors with TSA oversight instead of federal screening officers employed by the agency directly.

video:video.video:title: Gulf states call on UN Security Council to condemn 'Iran's unprovoked aggression' ahead of vote
video:video.video:description: The Council will vote on a draft resolution submitted by Bahrain on behalf of the Gulf Cooperation Council.

video:video.video:title: Authorities say ex-NFL player Darron Lee facing murder charge consulted AI bot before calling 911
video:video.video:description: Authorities testified in court that Darron Lee, a former NFL player who is charged with murder in his girlfriend's death in Tennessee, asked an AI bot for advice about the unresponsive woman before calling 911.

video:video.video:title: Each spring a rural corner of Catalonia turns pink as peach and nectarine trees blossom
video:video.video:description: Local officials say around 20,000 people visit the orchards near the village of Aitona in the Catalonia region, in northeastern Spain, each year during the brief flowering season of peach and nectarine trees.

video:video.video:title: UN Security Council demands Iran halt attacks on its Gulf neighbors
video:video.video:description: The 13-0 vote in the U.N.’s most powerful body reflects the isolated position Iran finds itself in, as it fires missiles and drones in retaliation for the ongoing U.S.

video:video.video:title: Ready set roll! Conan O'Brien unveils Oscars red carpet
video:video.video:description: Academy Awards host Conan O'Brien joined crew members to roll out the red carpet for Sunday's Oscars at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles. (March 11)

video:video.video:title: Small plane flips during takeoff in California
video:video.video:description: There were no injuries reported and the cause of the crash remains unclear, ABC affiliate KABC reported.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, March 11th: Trump tells America he's winning in Iran and is less clear on war's end; Iran targets ships, Dubai airport and oil facilities; Israel says Iran's new supreme leader was wounded; IEA agrees to release oil reserves.

video:video.video:title: British Prime Minister was warned of 'reputational risk' in Mandelson appointment
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was warned that Peter Mandelson ’s friendship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein exposed the government to “reputational risk,” but he still appointed him as ambassador to the United States, documents released Wednesday show.

video:video.video:title: Why some airports might replace TSA agents with private contractors
video:video.video:description: As TSA staffing shortages cause long airport lines nationwide, San Francisco International Airport’s private security model is drawing attention in a national debate.

video:video.video:title: Blaze at fuel storage tanks at Oman's Port of Salalah, amid days of Iranian attacks
video:video.video:description: Fire crews were working to contain a blaze Wednesday at the fuel storage tanks at Oman's Port of Salalah, amid days of Iranian attacks, according to the Oman News Agency.

video:video.video:title: Wealthy countries agree to largest-ever oil reserve release to counter price spike due to Iran war
video:video.video:description: The International Energy Agency has agreed to release the largest volume of emergency oil reserves in its history, in a bid to counter the effects on energy markets of the war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Israel strikes Gaza tent camp following evacuation order, sparking huge blaze
video:video.video:description: Israel strikes Gaza tent camp following evacuation order, sparking huge blaze

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike hits central Beirut's densely populated Aicha Bakkar area
video:video.video:description: Lebanon's Health Ministry said an Israeli airstrike on an apartment building in central Beirut early Wednesday wounded four people.

video:video.video:title: Debris strikes house in the West Bank as Iran fires missiles towards Israel
video:video.video:description: A home in the occupied West Bank was damaged by what locals said was debris from a missile. The shrapnel struck a home belonging to the Darwish family in the town of Biddya.

video:video.video:title: Trump speech in Kentucky interrupted by medical incident; Dr. Oz assists
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump’s speech in Hebron, Kentucky, was briefly interrupted when a rally-goer fainted behind him.

video:video.video:title: Injured helped into ambulances after Russian drone struck a moving public bus in Kherson
video:video.video:description: A Russian drone struck a moving public bus in Kherson Wednesday afternoon injuring ten people, including a 17-year-old boy, the head of the regional administration Oleksandr Prokudin said.

video:video.video:title: YouTuber Jake Paul joins Trump in Kentucky midterm rally
video:video.video:description: Youtuber Jake Paul also joined Trump onstage as the president visited Rep. Thomas Massie's Kentucky district hoping to see him defeated in an upcoming GOP primary.

video:video.video:title: Smoke rises from Irbil airport area in Iraq after drone attacks over the city
video:video.video:description: Drone attacks were launched late Wednesday toward the Iraqi Kurdistan region.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian company showcases interceptor drone designed to stop Shaheds
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian instructors from a private company demonstrated an interceptor drone developed to hunt down Shahed drones Wednesday in the Kyiv region as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a Ukrainian team was on its way to Gulf countries to help strengthen defenses as tensions escalate following Iranian

video:video.video:title: Seven killed and 21 wounded in Israeli strike on central Beirut
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese Health Ministry said an Israeli strike killed seven people and wounded 21 in the coastal area of Ramlet al-Bayda in central Beirut, early on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Kast takes office as Chile marks its sharpest shift to the right since the dictatorship
video:video.video:description: Chile’s ultraconservative José Antonio Kast took office as president Wednesday in what will mark the Latin American nation’s most pronounced shift to the right since the return of democracy in 1990.

video:video.video:title: 'We've won' Trump says of Iran war: 'They didn’t expect anything like this'
video:video.video:description: As the war with Iran rages on, President Donald Trump claimed Wednesday the U.S. has "won" the war, saying Tehran "didn’t expect anything like this."

video:video.video:title: Iran hosts public funeral for commanders killed on the first day of the war
video:video.video:description: Mourners and supporters of the Iranian government attended a funeral in Tehran on Wednesday for senior military commanders and others killed during the ongoing war with the United States and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Iranian attack triggers huge blaze at Bahrain’s Muharraq government facility
video:video.video:description: Bahrain Interior ministry released a video of a huge fire after Iranian attacks at a facility in Muharraq Governorate, northeast of the country’s capital, Manama.

video:video.video:title: South Africa deploys troops in Johannesburg to tackle organized crime
video:video.video:description: Soldiers were deployed to the streets of South Africa’s biggest city Wednesday in an effort to help police fight gang violence and illegal mining.

video:video.video:title: José Antonio Kast pledges his new presidency will combat crime and irregular migration
video:video.video:description: José Antonio Kast was sworn in on Wednesday as Chile’s new president and later pledged to implement what he described as an “emergency government” focused on combating crime and irregular migration.

video:video.video:title: Drug use is on the rise in Cuba, despite a zero-tolerance policy
video:video.video:description: Cuba is grappling with a fast-growing wave of synthetic drug use that is hitting young people hard. (AP Video by Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán

video:video.video:title: Cambodia aims to shut down all online scam centers there by the end of April
video:video.video:description: Cambodia hopes to shut down all of the country’s notorious online scam centers by the end of next month, the head of the Southeast Asian nation's effort to combat the cybercrime said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: As gas prices rise, electric vehicle owners have an advantage. Could that spark more EV interest?
video:video.video:description: Experts say prolonged high gas prices may drive some EV interest and sales.

video:video.video:title: Trump asked on possible Iran revenge plot in California: "It's being investigated"
video:video.video:description: A reporter asked President Donald Trump on Wednesday about a 'bulletin that some law enforcement put out about a possible Iran revenge plot in California.'
